title: Mobility Suppression Boundary Principle for Polymer Glass-Transition Temperature: A Public-Safe Cheminformatics Validation Study
description: Public-safe code, manuscript, figures, aggregate tables, tests, and reproducibility package for MSBP-Tg, a cheminformatics validation study of a mobility-suppression coordinate for polymer glass-transition temperature. MSBP density is defined as -NumRotatableBonds/heavy_atoms. The contribution is within-fiber descriptor-coordinate validation and public-safe reproducibility, not descriptor invention, not a universal Tg law, and not a replacement for experimental measurement. Raw and row-level third-party SMILES/Tg records are not redistributed.
notes: Journal-submission-ready public-safe software/archive package corresponding to the ChemRxiv preprint DOI https://doi.org/10.26434/chemrxiv.15005629/v1. The MIT license covers original code and documentation only; third-party data retain their original source terms and are not redistributed.
